author: Dr. Andre Williams
content:
PRIMARY CARE FOLLOW-UP — 12/22/2026, 11:15 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Interpreter: In-person Spanish-speaking MA.

INTERVAL EVENTS:
6 weeks since last seen at Esperanza (PCP, 9/15/2026); ~7 weeks since ED visit. Patient seen at Hopkins rheumatology 11/9/2026 (added mycophenolate), 11/30/2026 (continued; MMF increased to 1 g BID; pred tapered to 25 mg daily). MMF approved by PAP for 6 months. Has been adherent to all meds. Medicaid appeal was DENIED again 11/20/2026; family is exploring filing a second appeal with legal aid.

PATIENT REPORTS:
- Joint pain MUCH better. She can do most household tasks again. Returned to housekeeping work part-time (10-12 hr/week) for the past 2 weeks.
- Fatigue improving slowly but still substantial.
- Rash on chest mostly resolved.
- Weight: She has been able to eat better with prednisone. Weight up to 47.2 kg today (from 43.8 kg at ED). She believes she has gained back some of what she lost.
- Hair: Continues to shed but at slower rate.
- New: She mentions that for the past 2-3 weeks she has occasional difficulty swallowing dry foods, particularly bread and chicken — she feels like food 'gets stuck' briefly before going down. No coughing, choking, no nasal regurgitation. She has not had any food go down 'the wrong way.' Drinks liquids without issue. She has not lost weight from this; in fact she's gaining. She wonders if it's related to the medications.
- Mood: Better with treatment response. PHQ-9 today 7 (mild). Behavioral health intake completed; she is seeing a therapist every 2 weeks.
- Glucose: She has been checking AM glucose; reports values 105-130 mg/dL (consistent with steroid-induced hyperglycemia, not severe).
- Denies: Fever, chest pain, dyspnea, vision changes, focal weakness, abdominal pain (no MMF GI side effects), dark urine, leg swelling.

MEDS (verified): Mycophenolate 1g PO BID; Prednisone 25 mg PO daily (tapering); Hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID; Levothyroxine 25 mcg PO daily; Calcium 500 mg BID; Vitamin D 1000 IU daily; Omeprazole 20 mg daily. PRN tylenol. No NSAIDs.

VITALS: BP 108/66, HR 88, RR 14, T 98.4 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 47.2 kg (up 3.4 kg from ED 11/4/2026). Height 150 cm. BMI 21.0.

FOCUSED EXAM:
General: Looks much improved from 9/15/2026; less cachetic appearance, better color.
HEENT: Oropharynx clear, no thrush, no ulcers (carefully inspected). No cervical adenopathy. Thyroid normal.
Skin: Faint residual erythema on V-area but largely resolved. Diffuse scalp hair thinning, possibly slightly less than prior. No new rash.
CV: RRR, no murmurs. Lungs clear.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender.
MSK: Minimal synovitis. Wrists with trace tenderness; MCPs and PIPs no clear swelling today. Knees no effusion. Grip 4+/5 bilaterally. Proximal strength: 5/5 hip flexion, 5/5 shoulder abduction, 5/5 knee extension.
SWALLOW SCREEN: Asked patient to drink water (no cough, no delay), eat a small piece of bread (slight delay observed in the swallow — she paused, then completed; no coughing or distress; voice clear afterward), and swallow saliva on command (intact). No drooling. Voice normal. Bedside cough reflex intact. Cranial nerves 9/10/12 grossly intact on testing.

LABS (results from 12/15/2026 routine draw, reviewed today):
- CBC: WBC 4.10 (normal), Hgb 11.6 (low-normal), Plt 232 (normal)
- CMP: Cr 0.5, BUN 9, Albumin 3.5 (normal at lower limit, much improved), Glucose 122 (steroid-effect, fasting 98 last week)
- ESR 22 (much improved from 52), CRP 0.6 (improved from 2.3)
- Anti-dsDNA 14 (down from 24)
- C3 92 (now in normal range), C4 12 (normal)
- UPC 0.12 (now normal)
- TSH 3.42 (now in range)

ASSESSMENT:

23-year-old woman with active SLE responding well to treatment intensification (steroid + mycophenolate + HCQ) over 6 weeks. Lab markers improving substantially (ESR/CRP down, dsDNA decreasing, complements normalized, UPC normalized, albumin recovered). Weight gain 3.4 kg. Joint disease well-controlled clinically. Mood improving.

However, she reports a new symptom that warrants attention: occasional bolus dysphagia with dry foods over the past 2-3 weeks. Bedside swallow screen today was equivocal — I observed a slight delay in the swallow phase with bread, no choking. Strength preserved on exam (5/5 proximal and distal). No focal neurologic findings.

I will discuss with rheumatology by message today, get baseline labs, and reinforce return precautions for worsening dysphagia or any choking event.

PLAN:
1. Continue mycophenolate 1g BID, HCQ 200 mg BID, levothyroxine, calcium/vit D, omeprazole.
2. Continue prednisone taper per rheumatology plan: currently 25 mg daily, planned to decrease by 5 mg every 2 weeks. Next decrease 1/5/2027 to 20 mg.
3. ORDER: CBC, CMP, CK, aldolase, LDH given new symptom for baseline workup.
4. ORDER: Repeat anti-dsDNA, C3/C4, UPC, CBC, CMP — 4 weeks.
5. Reinforce dysphagia precautions: smaller bites, take time eating, sit upright, take liquids with solids, alert immediately if food gets fully stuck, choking, or coughing during meals. Avoid eating alone if symptoms worsen.
6. Message rheumatology (Dr. Tanaka) re: new bolus dysphagia, request guidance on whether to escalate evaluation (e.g., modified barium swallow, EMG).
7. Follow-up Esperanza in 4 weeks (target 1/19/2027) or sooner if symptoms worsen.
8. Continue Esperanza Behavioral Health follow-up.
9. Continue social work — second Medicaid appeal in progress.

Visit time: 40 minutes.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Andre Williams, MD
Esperanza Health (FQHC)
Date: 12/22/2026
